objective: Architect sign-off on the additive read-only verify_lock wasm sha256 primitive (LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) in core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
key_changes: Purely additive: no existing signatures, lock-file format, or Cargo.toml deps changed — nothing downstream must migrate.
Verifier reuses the same sha256() primitive as write_lock_for/wasm_sha256(), so a fresh lock round-trips to all-Match with no writer/verifier skew.
Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm is consistent with resolve(); covers dev-stubs, cache_root, GROVE_REGISTRY.
Signal mapping clean and non-swallowing: Ok(None) absent, NotFound->Missing, eq->Match, diff->Mismatch; other I/O errors propagate via anyhow.
Malformed lock entries deliberately continue-skipped, not unwrapped — resolves the plan-review 'decide deliberately' advisory.
No cross-cutting coupling; doc comment correctly directs T07 to pass the resolved grove.lock file path, not a project root.
Independently corroborated: code review 8/8 plan/AC + clippy clean; validation 5/5 AC pass, full suite green.
Deployment: no version bump on its own, no regeneration, no migration, no security scan.
Advisory (non-blocking): lock top-level version field not validated — acceptable for a read-only advisory primitive; fold into T07 if doctor ever gates on schema version.
Follow-up: T07 grove doctor is the intended consumer — surface Mismatch/Missing as actionable diagnostics.
